prompt:
あなたはTOEIC Part 6のテスト作成専門家です。
長文穴埋め問題を作成してください。

TOEIC Part 6の形式：
- 1つの文書（メール、記事、広告など）に4つの空欄
- 各空欄に4つの選択肢（A, B, C, D）
- 語彙・文法・文脈理解を総合的に評価
- ビジネス文書形式

重要な要求事項：
1. 自然で実用的なビジネス文書を作成
2. 文法的に正確で、ネイティブスピーカーが自然に感じる表現
3. TOEIC Part 6の形式と難易度に準拠
4. 4つの空欄を適切な位置に配置
5. 各空欄に4つの選択肢を提供
6. 正解は1つのみ、他は明確に不正解
7. 日本語解説も含める

出力形式はJSON形式で以下の構造にしてください：
{
  "documentType": "指定された文書タイプ",
  "title": "文書のタイトル",
  "fullText": "空欄を(1)、(2)、(3)、(4)で示した完全な文書",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": ["選択肢A", "選択肢B", "選択肢C", "選択肢D"],
      "correct": "A|B|C|D",
      "explanation": "なぜその答えが正しいかの説明（日本語）",
      "questionType": "vocabulary|grammar|context|sentence_insertion"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "easy|medium|hard",
  "topic": "ビジネス分野",
  "wordCount": 文書の単語数
}

以下の条件でTOEIC Part 6問題を1問作成してください：

【生成条件】
- 難易度: hard
- 文書タイプ: memo (Internal Memo)
- ビジネストピック: customer_inquiries (顧客問い合わせ)
- 空欄数: 4個

【特別な要求】
各空欄の正解位置は以下のように配置してください：
- 空欄1の正解位置: C
- 空欄2の正解位置: C
- 空欄3の正解位置: B
- 空欄4の正解位置: C

【問題タイプの配分】
- 空欄1: context (文脈理解)
- 空欄2: vocabulary (語彙選択)
- 空欄3: grammar (文法・語形)
- 空欄4: sentence_insertion (文挿入)

JSON形式で問題を作成してください。

{
  "documentType": "Internal Memo",
  "title": "Updated Procedure for Customer Inquiries",
  "fullText": "To: All Customer Support Staff\nFrom: Jane Morales, Customer Service Manager\nDate: August 18, 2025\nSubject: Updated Procedure for Customer Inquiries\n\nPlease review the following updates to our customer inquiry handling process, effective immediately. (1) Notably, our recent audit revealed inconsistencies in response times and escalation practices that have led to delayed resolutions.\n\nWhen a new inquiry is received, the first priority is to acknowledge receipt within one business hour. This initial acknowledgement should include confirmation that we have received the customer's message and an estimated timeframe for a full response. It is also important to assess the inquiry's urgency; low-priority issues may be queued, while those that threaten service continuity require immediate action and (2) engage the appropriate technical team for support.\n\nSupport agents should use the standardized response templates available in the Knowledge Base and customize them to the customer's specific situation. Use the escalation matrix to determine who should be copied on communications, and ensure that all steps taken are recorded in the ticket notes. If a ticket remains unresolved after two business days, the agent must escalate to a supervisor and (3) provide a summary of actions taken to date.\n\nAdditionally, we are introducing a weekly quality review to identify recurring issues and training needs. Please incorporate feedback from these reviews into your individual development plans.\n\n(4) As a related reminder, all correspondence with customers must adhere to the communication standards outlined in the employee handbook.\n\nMaintaining clear, courteous communication is paramount to preserving customer trust.\n\nThank you for your attention and cooperation.",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": [
        "For reference,",
        "In the meantime,",
        "Notably,",
        "Accordingly,"
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "文脈的に「最近の監査で〜が判明した」と続くため、話題を強調する接続語が適切です。Notably（特筆すべきことに）は audit の発見を自然に導くので正解。For reference（参考までに）は弱く、In the meantime（その間）は時間的対比を示し不適切、Accordingly（それゆえに）は因果関係が逆で不自然です。",
      "questionType": "context"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank2",
      "position": 2,
      "options": [
        "to notify of",
        "escalates",
        "engage",
        "alerting"
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "文は『require immediate action and ___ the appropriate technical team』という並列構造なので、動詞の原形が必要です。'engage the appropriate technical team'（適切な技術チームを連携させる／動員する）が語彙的にも意味的にも最適。A と D は形が不適切、B は三人称単数現在形で主語と合わず誤りです。",
      "questionType": "vocabulary"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank3",
      "position": 3,
      "options": [
        "provides",
        "provide",
        "provided",
        "to have provided"
      ],
      "correct": "B",
      "explanation": "構造は『must escalate ... and ___ a summary』で、助動詞 must に続く並列の動詞は原形で統一する必要があります。したがって 'provide' が正しい。'provides' は主語と一致せず、'provided' は過去分詞／過去形で意味が変わり、'to have provided' は不適切な不定詞形です。",
      "questionType": "grammar"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank4",
      "position": 4,
      "options": [
        "However, we will continue to monitor agent productivity closely.",
        "While supervisors oversee escalations, agents remain responsible for initial customer contact.",
        "As a related reminder, all correspondence with customers must adhere to the communication standards outlined in the employee handbook.",
        "Because of the increased volume, we plan to hire two additional agents next quarter."
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "挿入文は直後の文（\"Maintaining clear, courteous communication...\"）と論理的につながり、レビューや育成計画に続く注意喚起である必要があります。選択肢 C は「関連する注意」として自然に流れ、次の文の主題（顧客対応の明確さと礼儀）へ直接つながるため正しい。A は生産性監視の話題で脈絡がずれ、B は役割分担の説明で気持ちがそられる、D は人員計画の話題で不適切です。",
      "questionType": "sentence_insertion"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "topic": "customer_inquiries",
  "wordCount": 257
}

あなたはTOEIC Part 6問題の翻訳専門家です。
文書全体と選択肢を適切に日本語に翻訳してください。

重要なルール：
1. 文書は自然で分かりやすい日本語に翻訳
2. 文書の翻訳では、各空欄に正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳すること
3. 選択肢は1-4語の簡潔な日本語に翻訳
4. 選択肢に説明や解説は絶対に不要
5. 文脈に応じた最も適切な意味のみ
6. ビジネス用語は適切な日本語表現を使用

出力形式：
{
  "titleTranslation": "タイトルの翻訳",
  "contentTranslation": "文書全体の翻訳（各空欄に正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳）",
  "questionsTranslations": [
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    }
  ]
}

次のTOEIC Part 6問題を翻訳してください：

タイトル：Updated Procedure for Customer Inquiries

文書全体（空欄あり）：
To: All Customer Support Staff
From: Jane Morales, Customer Service Manager
Date: August 18, 2025
Subject: Updated Procedure for Customer Inquiries

Please review the following updates to our customer inquiry handling process, effective immediately. (1) Notably, our recent audit revealed inconsistencies in response times and escalation practices that have led to delayed resolutions.

When a new inquiry is received, the first priority is to acknowledge receipt within one business hour. This initial acknowledgement should include confirmation that we have received the customer's message and an estimated timeframe for a full response. It is also important to assess the inquiry's urgency; low-priority issues may be queued, while those that threaten service continuity require immediate action and (2) engage the appropriate technical team for support.

Support agents should use the standardized response templates available in the Knowledge Base and customize them to the customer's specific situation. Use the escalation matrix to determine who should be copied on communications, and ensure that all steps taken are recorded in the ticket notes. If a ticket remains unresolved after two business days, the agent must escalate to a supervisor and (3) provide a summary of actions taken to date.

Additionally, we are introducing a weekly quality review to identify recurring issues and training needs. Please incorporate feedback from these reviews into your individual development plans.

(4) As a related reminder, all correspondence with customers must adhere to the communication standards outlined in the employee handbook.

Maintaining clear, courteous communication is paramount to preserving customer trust.

Thank you for your attention and cooperation.

各空欄の正解：
空欄1: Notably,
空欄2: engage
空欄3: provide
空欄4: As a related reminder, all correspondence with customers must adhere to the communication standards outlined in the employee handbook.

選択肢：
空欄1: A. For reference,, B. In the meantime,, C. Notably,, D. Accordingly,
空欄2: A. to notify of, B. escalates, C. engage, D. alerting
空欄3: A. provides, B. provide, C. provided, D. to have provided
空欄4: A. However, we will continue to monitor agent productivity closely., B. While supervisors oversee escalations, agents remain responsible for initial customer contact., C. As a related reminder, all correspondence with customers must adhere to the communication standards outlined in the employee handbook., D. Because of the increased volume, we plan to hire two additional agents next quarter.

重要：文書の翻訳では、各空欄に上記の正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳してください。

JSON形式で翻訳を出力してください。
